reasoning: Iteration 2 regressed by 64.7ms (726.6ms vs 661.9ms iter 1) due to two critical issues: MADV_WILLNEED prefetching caused disk thrashing on HDD (+96ms orders scan regression), and FastIntHash added CPU overhead (+16.5ms customer scan regression). However, lineitem zone map block skipping worked excellently (-54.5ms join improvement). In this final iteration (remaining=0), we must reverse the failed optimizations and apply proven techniques from iteration 2's recommendations: fix the I/O strategy, eliminate hash function overhead, and apply high-impact optimizations (Abseil hash tables, Bloom filter removal, parallel merge). These fixes directly address the regression root causes while preserving the zone map wins.
focus_areas: Remove MADV_WILLNEED prefetching to restore HDD sequential I/O patterns (critical regression fix)
Remove FastIntHash and revert to std::hash to eliminate CPU overhead (critical regression fix)
Replace std::unordered_map/set with Abseil flat_hash_set/map for 2-5x faster lookups (SIMD-accelerated Swiss Tables)
Remove Bloom filter overhead (76.1ms) and rely on direct fast hash table probes
Implement parallel merge tree reduction to convert 46.3ms sequential merge to 8-12ms parallel merge
Keep lineitem zone map block skipping from iteration 2 - this optimization is sound and delivered 34% improvement to join phase
strategy_notes: This iteration focuses on FIXING REGRESSIONS and applying PROVEN TECHNIQUES: (1) Diagnose and reverse iteration 2's failures: revert MADV_WILLNEED prefetching (orders scan: 449.9ms → ~350-360ms), revert FastIntHash (customer scan: 46.8ms → ~30-35ms). Target: restore baseline to ~660ms or better. (2) Apply high-confidence optimizations: Abseil hash tables provide 2-5x speedup on membership tests and aggregations (orders scan: ~350ms → ~320-340ms, join_agg: 104ms → 80-90ms). (3) Remove Bloom filter overhead: 76.1ms build cost outweighs benefits for ~5-7% join selectivity; direct probes with fast hash table are superior (~-66ms net). (4) Parallelize merge phase: 46.3ms → ~8-12ms via tree reduction (~-35ms). (5) Expected trajectory: 726.6ms (iter 2) → ~370-425ms (all optimizations applied). This reaches ~4.7x speedup vs DuckDB baseline (78ms), narrowing the gap significantly. Even if some optimizations partially succeed, expected improvement is 100-150ms net positive. Per-operation timing shows orders scan (61.9%) and join/agg (14.3%) are dominant — focus optimization effort there.
notes: CRITICAL: Keep the lineitem zone map block skipping optimization from iteration 2—it is architecturally sound and delivered 34% improvement (158.5ms → 104.0ms). The regression was caused by I/O prefetching strategy and hash function overhead, NOT by block skipping logic. All 5 selected recommendations target bottlenecks identified in iteration 2's analysis and are lower-risk reversions/proven techniques rather than novel approaches. This is the final iteration (remaining=0), so this decision must maximize the remaining optimization budget.
